summary: Re-linked part of the Dolphin OS library by moving OSSerial and OSSync out of splits.todo.txt into the main GALE01 splits, marking several dolphin/os objects as Matching in configure.py, and tightening symbol scopes/names around OS exception and syscall vector labels. The source edits mostly separate real Metrowerks/Gekko inline assembly paths from non-Metrowerks placeholder implementations by switching many guards from MUST_MATCH to MWERKS_GEKKO and adding NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks where needed.
what_changed: Deleted config/GALE01/splits.todo.txt entries for dolphin/os/OSSerial.c and dolphin/os/OSSync.c that had been annotated with WARNING: ninja hangs forever.
Added dolphin/os/OSSerial.c and dolphin/os/OSSync.c to config/GALE01/splits.txt with their text/data/bss/sbss ranges, placing them before OSThread.c.
Changed configure.py dolphin/os object statuses from NonMatching to Matching for OSInit.c, OSAudioSystem.c, OSMemory.c, OSSync.c, and OSTime.c.
Removed separate configure.py entries for OSSerial_data.c, OSThread_data.c, and init/__ppc_eabi_init_data.c from the dolphin/os library object list.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to make several OS exception/vector boundary labels local rather than global, including __OSDBINTSTART, __OSDBINTEND, __OSDBJUMPEND, __OSEVStart, __OSSystemCallVectorStart, and __OSSystemCallVectorEnd.
Replaced the anonymous fn_8034AB80 symbol with a named local function __OSSystemCallVector at 0x8034AB80.

In OSInit.c, guarded exception-vector-related declarations and inline assembly with MWERKS_GEKKO rather than MUST_MATCH, used _stack_addr instead of _db_stack_end for debugArenaLo calculation, and added a NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback for OSExceptionInit outside MWERKS_GEKKO.
In OSMemory.c and OSTime.c, changed inline assembly function guards from MUST_MATCH to MWERKS_GEKKO.

In OSSync.c, made __OSSystemCallVectorStart and __OSSystemCallVectorEnd static declarations, guarded the inline assembly syscall vector and __OSInitSystemCall implementation with MWERKS_GEKKO, and added NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks for non-MWERKS_GEKKO builds.

In OSReboot.c, separated MWERKS_GEKKO-only PAD include/static storage from MUST_MATCH DVD include, converted __OSReboot to use the ASM macro with a NOT_IMPLEMENTED non-MUST_MATCH body, and changed Header/SaveStart/SaveEnd storage from unk_t to void*.
In OSReset.c, renamed an unused fallback parameter from '_' to arg0.
In placeholder.h, changed the Metrowerks NOT_IMPLEMENTED asm-nop condition from defined(__MWERKS__) && (MUST_MATCH || WIP) to defined(__MWERKS__) && !defined(BUGFIX).
smart_moves: The PR did not only flip configure.py objects to Matching; it also updated splits and symbols so the re-link had coherent section boundaries and symbol visibility.
Renaming fn_8034AB80 to __OSSystemCallVector and marking vector boundary labels local improved symbol fidelity while avoiding accidental global exports for labels that are implementation details.

Using MWERKS_GEKKO for inline assembly guards decoupled compiler/assembler availability from the broader MUST_MATCH state, allowing matching code paths to exist while still providing portable NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs.

Adding NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks for OSExceptionInit, __OSSystemCallVector, and __OSInitSystemCall kept non-Metrowerks or non-matching builds structurally compilable instead of relying on unavailable inline assembly.

Moving OSSerial and OSSync from splits.todo.txt into splits.txt appears to resolve prior relinking/tooling blockage noted as ninja hangs forever, though the PR has no body/comments explaining the exact root cause.
decomp_lessons: When re-linking a library slice, synchronize configure.py matching status, splits.txt ranges, and symbols.txt scopes/names in one change; changing only one layer can leave the build graph or linker view inconsistent.
Boundary labels emitted inside inline assembly should often be local symbols unless external code genuinely references them.
Replace generated fallback names like fn_8034AB80 with SDK-style or assembly-entry names once the role is known, especially for vector stubs and hand-written assembly regions.
Use compiler/target feature guards such as MWERKS_GEKKO for source that requires Metrowerks inline assembly, rather than overloading MUST_MATCH as a general assembly-availability switch.
For decomp source that cannot be implemented portably yet, explicit NOT_IMPLEMENTED functions are preferable to omitting definitions under preprocessor guards.

Data-only companion object files may need to be removed from configure.py when relinking consolidates or reclassifies Dolphin OS objects; verify section ownership before keeping old _data object entries.
naming_conventions: Use SDK-style double-underscore OS internal names for Dolphin OS vectors and internals, such as __OSSystemCallVector, __OSSystemCallVectorStart, __OSSystemCallVectorEnd, __OSDBINTSTART, __OSDBINTEND, and __OSEVStart.
Prefer meaningful parameter names in stubs over placeholder underscores when the codebase style or compiler permits, as shown by Reset(s32 arg0).
Use typed storage such as void* for pointer-like static scratch variables instead of unk_t when the pointer role is evident, as in OSReboot Header, SaveStart, and SaveEnd.
Keep vector boundary labels local in symbols.txt when they are assembly labels rather than API-visible functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed multiple inline assembly guards from MUST_MATCH to MWERKS_GEKKO in OSInit.c, OSMemory.c, OSSync.c, and OSTime.c.
Provided non-assembly NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback bodies outside MWERKS_GEKKO or MUST_MATCH so the files remain buildable when inline assembly is unavailable.
Used the ASM macro for __OSReboot with a non-MUST_MATCH fallback body and a preserved MUST_MATCH assembly body.
Kept comments noting peephole sensitivity: OSInit references a peephole bug around __OSFPRInit, and OSSync notes peephole off is needed to match __OSInitSystemCall.
Made __OSSystemCallVectorStart and __OSSystemCallVectorEnd static C declarations matching their local symbol treatment.
Used _stack_addr rather than _db_stack_end in OSInit's debugArenaLo calculation, likely aligning with the linker symbol expected after relinking.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching dolphin/os/OSSerial.c or OSSync.c to see whether the former ninja hang was fully resolved or only bypassed by relinking.
Search for subsequent changes to MWERKS_GEKKO versus MUST_MATCH guards to confirm this became the preferred inline assembly convention.
Check later symbols.txt updates around __OSSystemCallVector and OS exception labels for additional scope or naming corrections.
Investigate why OSAudioSystem.c was marked Matching in configure.py without appearing in this diff's source-file edits.
